items.title: Lava La Rue – “Prince Of Bologna”
items.summary: The latest artist to contribute to Speedy Wunderground’s singles series is London's Lava La Rue. For the new single "Prince Of Bologna," Lava La Rue was influenced by fragments of conversation overheard at the smoking area of a London venue. Taken out of context, these shards of convo become uniquely iridescent, beautiful and batshit crazy.

items.title: Tinashe Announces New Album Popstar: Hear “Crash Out”
items.summary: Not that long ago, Tinashe was a cult-beloved singer who was stuck in seemingly endless major-label purgatory, despite obvious talent and charisma, as well as a pretty decent little run of hits. Ever since she finally went independent a few years ago, she's been thriving. She scored a couple of excellent viral hits on her 2024 album, 2024's Quantum Baby, and now she's following it with another one called Popstar.
items.audio: audio/Tinashe Announces New Album Popstar_ Hear _Crash O.mp3

items.title: Carly Rae Jepsen – “After All”
items.summary: Around the time that this summer draws to an end, cult-pop overlord Carly Rae Jepsen will return with Day And Night, a new 24-song double album divided into two distinct and self-explanatory halves. Jepsen has already shared lead single "On Wires," which comes from the daytime half of the record. Now, she's got another new one, and it's also from the day disc.

items.title: William Shatner’s metal band making its live debut at Riot Fest
items.summary: William Shatner and his heavy metal band The *uckers play the final day of Riot Fest on September 20. He’s got a metal album on the way too.

items.title: Lana Del Rey says new LP ‘Stove’ has a “companion album”
